rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Classify HOGs by clique conservation pattern

Source: R/cliques.R
classify_cliques.Rd

Convenience wrapper that runs find_cliques internally (once for all species, once per trait group) and applies a sequential waterfall classification. For fine-grained control over the clique detection parameters per step, call find_cliques() directly.

classify_cliques(edges, ...)

# Default S3 method
classify_cliques(
  edges,
  target_species,
  species_trait,
  min_species = 2L,
  max_genes_per_sp = 10L,
  max_missing_edges = 0L,
  edge_type = "conserved",
  stability = NULL,
  sweep = NULL,
  min_stability_class = 0L,
  min_persistence = 1,
  ...
)

Arguments

edges

Data frame with columns gene1, gene2, species1, species2, hog, q.value, effect_size, and type. Must contain ALL edges (conserved + ns + diverged), not pre-filtered, because the differentiated check needs to verify absence of cross-group conserved edges.

...

Additional arguments passed to the default method.

target_species

Character vector of all species.

species_trait

Named character or factor vector mapping each species to a trait value (e.g., c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "perennial")).

min_species

Minimum species for a partial or within-group clique (default 2).

max_genes_per_sp

Passed to find_cliques (default 10).

max_missing_edges

Passed to find_cliques for partial detection (default 0).

edge_type

Edge types considered conserved (default "conserved").

stability

Optional output of clique_stability.

sweep

Optional output of clique_threshold_sweep.

min_stability_class

Minimum stability class for the robust flag (default 0).

min_persistence

Minimum persistence for the robust flag (default 1.0).

Value

A data frame with one row per HOG:

hog

HOG identifier

classification

One of "complete", "partial", "differentiated", "trait_specific", "unclassified"

n_species

Species count in the best clique (NA for unclassified)

best_mean_q

Mean q-value of the best clique (NA for unclassified)

trait_groups

Comma-separated trait groups with internal cliques (NA for complete/partial/unclassified)

stability_class

From stability results (NA if not provided)

persistence

Highest multiplier at which the HOG's clique survived (NA if not provided)

robust

Logical: passes both stability and persistence thresholds (NA if neither provided)

Details

Each HOG is assigned to exactly one category; earlier categories take precedence.

The pipeline:

  1. complete: all target species form a clique (all C(N,2) edges conserved).

  2. partial: a clique exists with min_species to N-1 species (or with missing edges when max_missing_edges > 0).

  3. differentiated: at least 2 trait groups each have a within-group clique, but no cross-group conserved edge exists.

  4. trait_specific: exactly 1 trait group has a within-group clique.

  5. unclassified: none of the above.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
result <- classify_cliques(edges, target_species, species_trait)
table(result$classification)
} # }

Contents
